AdsArmor | Integrated System

Click Fraud — Attack & Defense

An integrated simulation platform demonstrating two real-world click fraud attack vectors against PPC advertising systems, and the three-layer ML defense architecture that counters them.

Threat 1

Competitive click fraud — 3 IPs, medium cadence, budget drain

Threat 2

Botnet + IP theft — machine speed, identity spoofing

Layer 1

Rate limiting & challenge — early obvious abuse filter

Layer 2

Behavioural ML — fraud scoring via Isolation Forest

Layer 3

Budget control — block, withhold, cap, charge actions

Layer 1 role

Filters obvious high-frequency abuse before deeper behavioural analysis. Uses rate-limiting and IP reputation to remove trivial fraud.

Layer 2 role

Transforms click-level behaviour (interval, dwell, concentration) into a continuous fraud score per IP, enabling interpretable risk levels.

Layer 3 role

Converts risk scores to billing actions: charge (legitimate), withhold (suspicious), cap_block (medium risk), or block (high risk).

Data integrity statement

Metrics above are cross-validated between click-level logs (N=253) and IP behavioural profiles (N=39). The £110.00 withheld amount represents suspicious activities flagged by Layer 2 behavioural analysis and withheld by the downstream billing logic pending manual audit.

ML core logic
Input click data stream
↓
Behavioural feature extraction
↓
Rule-based scoring + Isolation Forest
↓
Final fraud score (0.0 – 1.0)
↓
Risk level assignment
Aggregated: Transforms click-level logs into IP-level behavioural profiles.
Detection: Combines interpretable rules with anomaly detection.
Output & decision mapping
Low riskAllow
Medium riskFlag for review
High riskBlock & withhold

Risk classification determines the downstream billing mitigation strategy in Layer 3.

Fraud score distribution by IP

Empirical distribution of fraud scores across 39 analysed IP profiles. (Click to zoom)

Key insights

Layer 2 identifies suspicious behavioural patterns not removed by Layer 1. By transforming signals into a continuous fraud score, it improves interpretability and supports downstream billing protection.

Trade-off

Higher sensitivity improves fraud protection but may affect a small amount of high-frequency normal traffic near the decision boundary.
